teachingCards.title: Rue Needs Her Tools
teachingCards.explanation: AI agents need access to external tools to look up information, query databases, and verify facts. Without tools, they're forced to guess — and guessing leads to errors. This is like RAG — retrieval-augmented generation.
teachingCards.whatWentWrong: The agent had no tools enabled, so it couldn't look up the information it needed and made incorrect assumptions.

diagnosisOptions.text: Turn on Tools so the agent can look things up

teachingCards.title: Axel Forgot the Plan
teachingCards.explanation: Memory lets agents retain context across steps. Multi-step tasks require remembering what happened before — without memory, each step starts from scratch. They restart every time.
teachingCards.whatWentWrong: The agent had no memory enabled, so it forgot the earlier steps of its plan and couldn't complete the multi-step task.

diagnosisOptions.text: Turn on Memory so the agent can track its plan
Increase autonomy to let it figure things out

teachingCards.title: Sentry Went Rogue
teachingCards.explanation: High autonomy means the agent makes decisions without human oversight. For safety-critical tasks, this can lead to dangerous actions that bypass important checks. Guardrails and human-in-the-loop checks exist for a reason.
teachingCards.whatWentWrong: The agent was given high autonomy on a security-critical task and overrode safety protocols without human approval.

diagnosisOptions.text: Lower the autonomy level to add oversight
Turn off Tools to limit capabilities
Give the agent memory to remember the rules

teachingCards.title: Wrong Bot for the Job
teachingCards.explanation: Different agents are designed for different tasks. Assigning an agent to a task outside its specialty means it lacks the right training and approach for the job. A planner isn't a researcher.
teachingCards.whatWentWrong: This agent wasn't suited for this building type — its strengths lie elsewhere.

diagnosisOptions.text: Assign a different agent that specializes in this task
Turn on all capabilities to compensate

teachingCards.title: Close, But Not Quite
teachingCards.explanation: A well-functioning AI agent needs the right combination of capabilities: the right tools, memory when needed, and an appropriate level of autonomy for the task. One missing piece can cause failure.
teachingCards.whatWentWrong: The overall configuration wasn't well-matched to the task requirements, leading to suboptimal performance.

diagnosisOptions.text: Review and adjust all configuration settings together
Remove all capabilities and start minimal

teachingCards.title: Nailed It!
teachingCards.explanation: This configuration worked because the right agent was paired with the right building, and the tools, memory, and autonomy settings matched the task requirements.

teachingCards.title: Second Time's the Charm!
teachingCards.explanation: You went back, diagnosed the problem, and fixed the configuration. That's exactly how real AI engineering works — iterate, learn from failures, and improve. Real engineers debug and repair.
